title: Oregon Department of Environmental Quality Proposes Rulemaking to Update GHG Measurement under the Clean Fuels Program
content: On 4th October, 2024, the Oregon Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) proposed rulemaking to bring in modifications to requirements and tools used to measure GHG emissions under the Stateâ€™s Clean Fuels Program. The DEQ has invited public comments on the proposed changes till 21st November 2024 prior to which a public hearing will be held on 19th November 2024. The proposed changes are focused on updates to OR-GREET model to calculate carbon intensity (CI) values, to be in line with the methodology followed by the Californian LCFS program. Further, the proposed rules bring in modifications to requirements for high-risk fuel pathways in addition to bringing increased third party verification and reporting requirements.
author: Analysts Account
